lines: The physiological sigh. [short pause] A Stanford study in 2023 found this is the single most effective real-time breathing technique for reducing stress — more effective than box breathing, meditation breathing, or mindfulness alone. [short pause] It works by fully inflating your lung's alveoli on the double inhale, then offloading excess CO2 on the long exhale, which directly activates your parasympathetic nervous system.
Two quick inhales through the nose. [short pause] One long exhale through the mouth. [short pause] Six cycles. [short pause] I'll guide you.
Short inhale through the nose... [short pause] [short pause] and a deeper second inhale... [short pause] [short pause]
Now a long, slow exhale through the mouth... [short pause] [short pause] let it all out.
Short inhale... [short pause] [short pause] deeper second inhale... [short pause] [short pause]
Long, slow exhale... [short pause] [short pause] empty your lungs completely.
Short inhale... [short pause] [short pause] deeper inhale on top... [short pause] [short pause]
Exhale slowly... [short pause] [short pause] all the way out.
Short inhale... [short pause] [short pause] second deeper inhale... [short pause] [short pause]
And release... [short pause] [short pause] long exhale through the mouth.
Short inhale... [short pause] [short pause] deep inhale... [short pause] [short pause]
Slow exhale... [short pause] [short pause] let everything go.
Short inhale... [short pause] [short pause] deeper second inhale... [short pause] [short pause]
Long, slow exhale... [short pause] [short pause] all the way out.
Six cycles done. [short pause] Breathe normally. [short pause] Notice the shift.
